name: TC4 Infusion Renderer + Enchantment Output Parity
fixed: Infusion Matrix block now uses an animated block-entity renderer instead of a fake cube_all/static block model.

Added InfusionMatrixRenderer with original infuser.png texture, eight 0.45-scale cubelets, +/-0.25 offsets, startup yaw/pitch/roll, instability wobble, translucent glow pass and a crafting halo adapter.
Runtime InfusionRecipe now carries TC4 recipeType, enchantment output id, optional NBTBase output label/tag and catalyst wildcard semantics.
Added TC4InfusionEnchantmentAdapter to materialize original InfusionEnchantmentRecipe entries into recipeType==1 runtime recipes.
Enchantment recipes now use central item wildcard matching, enchantment applicability, max-level and compatibility checks before recipe selection.
Enchantment aspect costs are scaled with the original getEssentiaMod behavior: current target enchant level plus 0.1 per other enchantment level.
Enchantment instability uses the original sum(existing enchant levels)/2 + recipe instability formula.
Enchantment XP uses the original recipeXP*(1+current target level) formula and drains one player level per cycle before essentia.
XP drain searches players within radius 10, skips level cost for creative players, applies magic damage 0..1 and emits source beam particles toward the matrix.
craftingFinish now supports recipeType==1 enchantment outputs by incrementing the existing enchantment on the central pedestal item.
craftingFinish now has an NBTBase-style adapter path through ItemStack.addTagElement(label, tag) for recipes that output labelled tags.
Component validation and delayed component pull now use recipe.componentMatches instead of raw item-id-only matching; container items are preserved through getCraftingRemainingItem.
Matrix NBT now saves/loads TC4 recipexp/recipetype aliases alongside modern RecipeXP/RecipeType fields.
still_not_full_original: Renderer is an adapter-grade recreation of TileRunicMatrixRenderer/ModelCube geometry, not a direct GL11 port; exact blend-state and per-face UV parity may still differ.
PacketFXInfusionSource and PacketFXBlockZap are still represented with server particle helpers rather than full packet classes.
Component matching is recipe-aware but still lacks complete 1.7.10 damage/OreDictionary/NBT tag equivalence for all original component variants.
Runic augment recipes still need full dynamic NBT/material component parity from InfusionRunicAugmentRecipe.
Failure effects still need exact original weighted event table 0..20, flux goo/gas parity, warp/sticky warp split and block zap effects.
Custom Thaumcraft enchantments such as Repair/Haste require their 1.19.2 enchantment registrations before those two recipes can function.
